evals.prompt: Run the quality gates on this frontend service. It's a Node project whose package.json exposes lint, build, test, and test:ui scripts, and there's a pnpm-lock.yaml checked in at the root.
evals.expected_output: Detects pnpm from the pnpm-lock.yaml lockfile, builds a gate plan covering lint, build, unit, affected, and the conditional UI gate (triggered by the test:ui script), executes the gates in sequence, and writes the outcome to qa-report.md.

assertions.name: lockfile-resolves-pnpm
assertions.description: Presence of pnpm-lock.yaml selects pnpm as the runner rather than any other Node package manager.

assertions.name: report-artifact-emitted
assertions.description: A qa-report.md file is produced summarizing the outcome of every gate.

assertions.name: ui-gate-gated-on-script
assertions.description: The conditional UI gate is added to the plan because a test:ui script exists in package.json.

evals.prompt: This repo ships both a package.json and a Cargo.toml at the root. Run the gates and tell me which toolchain you ended up using.
evals.expected_output: Selects the first matching runner per the documented detection precedence unless a quality-gates guide overrides that choice, runs the gate sequence, and marks any referenced-but-missing scripts as skipped rather than failing them.

assertions.name: precedence-picks-single-runner
assertions.description: With multiple manifests present, exactly one runner is chosen by the documented precedence, and a quality-gates guide is honored when it overrides that selection.

assertions.name: absent-script-marked-skipped
assertions.description: A gate whose script is referenced but not defined is reported as skipped, never as a failed gate.

evals.prompt: Run qa-gates here. Heads up: there is no package.json, no Cargo.toml, no pyproject.toml, no go.mod, and no quality-gates guide anywhere in this checkout.
evals.expected_output: Reports the runner as unknown without assuming npm, and asks the user exactly once to supply lint, build, and test commands or to grant permission to skip the gates.

assertions.name: no-silent-npm-default
assertions.description: With no manifest, lockfile, or quality-gates guide, the runner is reported as unknown and npm is not silently assumed.

assertions.name: single-clarification-prompt
assertions.description: The user is prompted a single time for lint/build/test commands or for permission to skip, rather than the skill proceeding blindly.
